title: 0-1 Knapsack
hints: This is a classic 0-1 knapsack problem where each item can be taken at most once.
Use dynamic programming: dp[w] represents the maximum value achievable with capacity w.
For each item, iterate backwards through capacities from C down to the item's weight.
For each capacity w, decide whether to include the current item: dp[w] = max(dp[w], dp[w - B[i]] + A[i]).
Iterating backwards ensures we don't use the same item twice in one iteration.
The answer will be dp[C], the maximum value achievable with full capacity.
